description_long: This government building features a stepped, tiered structure reminiscent of ancient ziggurats, with four main levels decreasing in size as they ascend. The exterior is clad in light-colored stone or concrete blocks, with a prominent tiled roof capping the uppermost tier. The front facade is distinguished by a long colonnade of arches, providing a sheltered walkway and a sense of grandeur. The building sits on a large, paved plaza with a geometric pattern, enhancing its monumental presence. The overall design is symmetrical, with each tier set back from the one below, and the main entrance is centrally located beneath the colonnade. There are no visible windows or decorative elements beyond the arches and roof tiles, giving the structure a formal, institutional appearance.
description: A monumental, tiered government building with a colonnaded front, light stone exterior, and a tiled roof, set on a large patterned plaza. Its stepped design and symmetrical form evoke a sense of authority and permanence.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stepped tiers, tiled roof, and the patterned plaza extending to the right.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the long colonnade of arches, central entrance, and symmetrical tiered structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with stepped tiers and roof visible, plaza extending to the left.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the plain, tiered back of the building with no visible entrances or arches.
attribute_public_entrance_design: The main public entrance features a grand colonnade of arches spanning the width of the building, providing a sheltered walkway and a formal, imposing approach. The entrance is centrally located and accessible from the large, open plaza.
